schema_purpose: This file lists trip-critical risks that the app must surface prominently (e.g. a red banner, not just buried in reference notes). Check this file for alerts matching the user's current or upcoming waypoint, and display any 'critical' severity alert unmissably -- on the map, in the day's itinerary view, and ideally as a push/local notification a day before the affected waypoint if the app supports scheduling against dates.

alerts.title: Possible transport gap: Kebnats -> Vakkotavare, around 6 September 2026 (see direction note in details)
alerts.short_summary: The only bus connecting the Kebnats boat pier onward to Vakkotavare may not run on 6 September 2026. Missing it strands you at Kebnats, unable to continue north to Vakkotavare that day. The confirmed gap dates below are for the Vakkotavare -> Kebnats direction; whether the same gap applies to the Kebnats -> Vakkotavare direction actually needed here is unconfirmed -- see details.
alerts.trip_context: Based on a south-to-north start from Kvikkjokk on 1 September 2026, arrival at Kebnats (km 81.4, via the boat from Saltoluokta) is estimated around 6-10 September 2026, depending on pace and rest days -- this estimate itself is not precise, which is part of the risk.
alerts.details: Bus operator Road to Ritsem runs two daily trips (Tur 1 and Tur 2) in season. The only timetable found so far is for the Vakkotavare -> Kebnats direction (heading out toward Gällivare); their date ranges do not overlap around early September: Tur 1 (Vakkotavare departure 14:40) stops running after 5 September and resumes 14 September. Tur 2 (Vakkotavare departure 09:20) only starts running (in this direction) 7 September. This leaves 6 September with no scheduled Vakkotavare -> Kebnats departure under either trip as currently published. IMPORTANT: this is a south-to-north hike, so the leg actually needed is the opposite direction, Kebnats -> Vakkotavare (heading further into the mountains) -- its timetable has not been found yet, so it's unconfirmed whether the same 6 September gap applies to it, though it seems likely since both directions share the same round-trip vehicle. The operator's own site also warns that 2026 train-maintenance disruption in Gällivare is causing irregular bus operation this summer, so even the printed schedule may not be reliable on other nearby dates either.
alerts.why_it_matters: Vakkotavare has no shop (per route.json shop data) and is not on a through-road with easy alternative transport. Missing the only bus of the day means an unplanned extra night with no resupply at Kebnats, and no guaranteed way to reach Vakkotavare or continue the hike that day.
action_required: As the hike progresses, re-estimate the actual arrival date at Kebnats and check whether it lands on or near 6 September 2026.

Before relying on this leg, get the actual Kebnats -> Vakkotavare (inbound) timetable -- not just the Vakkotavare -> Kebnats one currently in bus_vakkotavare_kebnats.json -- from roadtoritsem.com or by calling the operator.
Re-check bus_vakkotavare_kebnats.json close to departure (schedules and disruption notices can change).
If the estimated arrival date is 6 September or uncertain, consider building in a deliberate rest day earlier in the trip to shift arrival to 7 September or later, or to before 5 September.
Call Road to Ritsem directly (+46 970 248 00) once a firm date is known, to confirm that day's actual operation in the Kebnats -> Vakkotavare direction.
As a backup, the STF contacts for arranging transport outside the timetable (Christer Dynesius +46 70 241 66 35, Tobbe Öberg +46 70 340 42 72) may be able to advise on alternatives on the day.

alerts.title: STF Alesjaure Fjällstuga closes for the season around 13 September 2026
alerts.short_summary: Alesjaure's staffed season ends around 13 September 2026. Arriving after that means no host, no shop, and possibly no staffed service at all -- despite Alesjaure normally having STF's largest fjällstuga and a large shop on this route.
alerts.trip_context: Alesjaure is at km 155.4 from Kvikkjokk, 74 km past Vakkotavare (km 81.4, see the vakkotavare_kebnats_bus_gap_sep2026 alert for the same trip's early-September pacing risk at that earlier point). Re-estimate the actual arrival date at Alesjaure as the hike progresses -- if the pace implied by that earlier alert (arriving Vakkotavare around 6-10 September) holds, 13 September at Alesjaure is plausible but not comfortably certain.
alerts.details: The 13 September 2026 season-end date was provided directly by the route author, not yet independently confirmed against an official STF source for Alesjaure specifically. It is consistent with the same date already found elsewhere in this route's data for other staffed points: boat_aktse_laitaure.json notes cabins close and hosts leave around lunchtime on 13 September, and boat_sitojaure_svijnne.json notes only one lake crossing is available that day. This suggests 13 September 2026 is a general STF season-end date across multiple staffed points on this route, not an Alesjaure-only quirk -- but the Alesjaure-specific date should still be verified directly.
alerts.why_it_matters: Alesjaure has a large shop and is STF's largest fjällstuga on this route (78 beds) -- a resupply point the itinerary may be relying on. After the season-closing date, the shop and staffed host are very likely unavailable (huts commonly switch to unstaffed self-service mode after the staffed season, if usable at all), so relying on food or help there past this date is risky.
action_required: Confirm STF Alesjaure Fjällstuga's exact 2026 closing date and post-season status directly with STF (see phone numbers on the nearby staffed stations, or svenskaturistforeningen.se) before relying on its shop.
As the hike progresses, re-estimate the actual arrival date at Alesjaure and check it against the confirmed closing date.
If arrival is expected on or after the closing date, plan resupply around Sälka or Abiskojaure instead, and don't count on Alesjaure's shop or host.
